description: Promotional offer pattern where customers purchase multiple units of a product to receive an additional unit free, commonly used in F&B to drive bulk purchases and increase order value.
structural_recipe: Lead with excitement (✨ emojis, 'لا يفوتك'). State the offer clearly: 'اشترِ [quantity] وخذ [quantity] مجانًا'. Specify the product category. Include limited-time frame (dates or duration). Add CTA with website/link. Use emoji liberally for visual hierarchy. Include exclusivity language ('حصريًا').
why_it_works: Saudi consumers respond to value-stacking and bundled offers that feel like winning a deal. The 'free' component triggers psychological win-feeling and reduces price resistance. Time-limited frame creates urgency. Product specificity (Ethiopian capsules/coffee) builds trust and clarity.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure promotional terms comply with Saudi SFDA regulations for F&B claims
Verify inventory can support anticipated bulk purchase surge
Time-limited offers must have realistic duration; vague deadlines reduce credibility in Saudi market
Free item must be same or similar product category to avoid consumer confusion
cultural_constraints: Use Arabic-first copywriting; English secondary at best
Align promotional period with Saudi shopping seasons (Ramadan, national holidays, back-to-school)
Avoid timing conflicts with religious observances
